summary: Datetime Timezone Fix Verified - All 12 tests passed. The fix for datetime comparison errors between offset-naive and offset-aware datetimes in design_approval_gate.py is working correctly.
passed_tests: test_02_create_design_submission - POST /api/projects/{id}/design-submissions works without datetime errors

bug_fix_verification.issue: Design submissions were failing due to datetime comparison errors between offset-naive and offset-aware datetimes
bug_fix_verification.root_cause: In design_approval_gate.py, deadline dates were being compared as offset-naive vs offset-aware datetimes, causing TypeError
fix_applied: create_submission_document() (lines 140-175): Added timezone awareness to deadline parsing with .replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
calculate_deadline_status() (lines 216-248): Added timezone awareness to deadline parsing with .replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
evidence: Created submission DS-72ac9bee3707 without datetime errors
Submission appeared in review queue correctly
Approved submission successfully - status changed to 'approved'
Full flow: Designer submits → Manager sees → Approves → Milestone unlocks - all working

critical_code_review_comments: Timezone fix correctly applied - all datetime comparisons now use timezone-aware datetimes
create_submission_document() properly handles various deadline formats (ISO with T, date-only YYYY-MM-DD)
calculate_deadline_status() properly handles various deadline formats with fallback to has_deadline: false
Both functions ensure timezone awareness with .replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc) for naive datetimes
seed_data_creation: Created test submission DS-72ac9bee3707 (approved) during testing on project proj_e4f505cf
context_for_next_testing_agent: Datetime timezone fix verified. Key endpoints tested: POST /api/projects/{id}/design-submissions, GET /api/design-manager/review-queue, PUT /api/projects/{id}/design-submissions/{id}/review. Test file test_datetime_timezone_fix.py can be used for regression testing.

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: In design_approval_gate.py, the create_submission_document() and calculate_deadline_status() functions were comparing datetime.now(timezone.utc) (offset-aware) with deadline dates parsed without timezone info (offset-naive), causing TypeError: can't compare offset-naive and offset-aware datetimes
rca_of_the_issue.impact: Design submissions would fail when deadline dates were present, preventing designers from submitting designs for approval
rca_of_the_issue.fix: Added timezone awareness to deadline parsing in both functions: 1) Parse deadline string to datetime, 2) Check if tzinfo is None, 3) If naive, add UTC timezone with .replace(tzinfo=timezone.utc)
rca_of_the_issue.verification: All 12 tests pass - submission creation, review queue, approval, and status verification all working correctly without datetime errors
